channel.title: Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Manage SQL database schemas in VS Code: Publish dialog and item templates
item.description: Making schema changes often means jumping between tools. You write code in VS Code, then switch to a separate tool to deploy your changes : exporting a script, running it manually, or copy-pasting into a query editor. Either way, it pulls you out of your flow. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sql/sql-database-projects-publish-dialog-item-templates

item.title: Agentic Platform Engineering with GitHub Copilot
item.description: We've talked about the human scale problem and what happens when infrastructure scales but understandin Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/all-things-azure/agentic-platform-engineering-with-github-copilot

item.title: Generative AI for Beginners .NET: Version 2 on .NET 10
item.description: Today we're releasing Version 2 of Generative AI for Beginners .NET, our free, open-source course for building AI-powered .NET applications. If you want to understand and build generative AI applications, from core concepts to production-ready patterns, this is you Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/generative-ai-for-beginners-dotnet-version-2-on-dotnet-10

item.title: From code to cloud: Deploy an AI agent to Microsoft Foundry in minutes with azd
item.description: You built an AI agent. It works locally. Now what? Getting an agent from your laptop to a production endpoint on Microsoft Foundry usually means stitching together resource provisioning, model deployments, identity configuration, and connection wiring—and that doesn't even cover monitoring or debugging. What if you could go fro Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/azd-ai-agent-end-to-end

item.title: Announcing TypeScript 6.0
item.description: Today we are excited to announce the availability of TypeScript 6.0! If you are not familiar with TypeScript, it's a language that builds on JavaScript by adding syntax for types, which enables type-checking to catch errors, and provide rich editor tooling. You can learn more about TypeScript and how to get started on the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-6-0

item.title: Announcing Aspire 13.2
item.description: Aspire 13.2 is here — and this one's a big deal. TypeScript AppHost authoring. An AI-agent-native CLI. New and improved integrations. A smarter dashboard. We've been building toward some of these for a while, and today they ship. [cta-button align='center' text='Get Aspire 13.2' url='https://get.aspire.dev'] Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/aspire/aspire-13-2-announcement

item.title: Ten Months with Copilot Coding Agent in dotnet/runtime
item.description: When GitHub's Copilot Coding Agent (CCA) first became available in May 2025, the promise of cloud-based AI coding agents was generating enormous excitement, and equal skepticism. So we began an experiment, and like many experiments in software enginee Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/ten-months-with-cca-in-dotnet-runtime

item.title: How can I make sure the anti-malware software doesn't terminate my custom service?
item.description: A customer was developing a Windows service process, and it is important to them that the service keep running on their servers. They wanted to know if there was a way they could prevent users who connect to the server from terminating the service. In particular, they wanted to make sure that the user couldn't use the anti-malware software Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260323-00/?p=112157

item.title: Important updates: GitHub Copilot support ending for JetBrains 2024.2 and 2024.3
item.description: To deliver new features faster and continue improving stability and performance, GitHub Copilot plugin will end support for JetBrains IDEs versions 2024.2 and 2024.3. By focusing on newer versions, we can bring you new capabilities more quickly and ensure a high-quality experience. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/java/important-updates-ghsupport-ending-for-jetbrains-2024-2-and-2024-3

item.title: Windows stack limit checking retrospective: arm64, also known as AArch64
item.description: Our survey of stack limit checking wraps up with arm64, also known as AArch64. The stack limit checking takes two forms, one simple version for pure arm64 processes, and a more complex version for Arm64EC. I'm going to look at the simple version. The complex version differs in that it has to check whether the code is running on the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260320-00/?p=112154

item.title: Accelerating .NET MAUI Development with AI Agents
item.description: This is a guest blog from Syncfusion. Learn more about the free, open-source Syncfusion Toolkit for .NET MAUI. As a proud partner with the .NET MAUI team, Syncfusion is excited to share how custom-built AI agents are dramatically improvin Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/accelerating-dotnet-maui-with-ai-agents

item.title: Remote MCP Server preview in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Earlier this week we release the public preview for our Azure DevOps MCP Server. Today we are excited to let you know that the Azure DevOps MCP Server is now available to use in Microsoft Foundry. For those who are new to Foundry, Micro Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/remote-mcp-server-preview-in-microsoft-foundry

item.title: Windows stack limit checking retrospective: amd64, also known as x86-64
item.description: Our survey of stack limit checking reaches the modern day with amd64, also known as x86-64. This time, there are two versions of the function, one for user mode and one for kernel mode. We'll look at the user mode version. Actually, there are two user mode versions. One is in msvcrt, the legacy runtime. ; on entry Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260319-00/?p=112152

item.title: From Azure IoT Operations Data Processor Pipelines to Dataflows
item.description: We recently completed an 18-month journey building an event detection system at the edge with Azure IoT Operations (AIO). The goal was simple to describe but complex to execute: use a Vision Model to detect real-world events, then raise confidence by correlating that signal with other data sources—from indu Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/aio-data-processor-pipelines-to-dataflow-processing-data-in-the-edge

item.title: Announcing PowerShell 7.6 (LTS) GA Release
item.description: We're excited to announce the General Availability of PowerShell 7.6, the next Long Term Support (LTS) release of PowerShell. PowerShell 7.6 is built on .NET 10 (LTS), continuing the alignment between PowerShell and the modern .NET platform. PowerShell 7.6 includes reliability improvements across the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/powershell/announcing-powershell-7-6

item.title: Authentication Tokens Are Not a Data Contract
item.description: Authentication tokens exist to answer one question: is this caller authorized to do this? They are not intended to be a stable data interface, a schema you can depend on, or an input into application logic. If your application decodes tokens and reads claims from them, this is an important heads-up. Token C Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/authentication-tokens-are-not-a-data-contract

item.title: Pin Clustering in .NET MAUI Maps
item.description: If you've ever loaded a map with dozens — or hundreds — of pins, you know the result: an overlapping mess that's impossible to interact with. Starting in .NET MAUI 11 Preview 2, the Map control supports pin clustering out of the box on Android and iOS/Mac Catalyst. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/pin-clustering-in-dotnet-maui-maps

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd
item.description: You can now run an AI agent on your local machine and send it messages—all from azd, no portal required. What's new? The azure.ai.agents extension adds two commands: Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/azd-ai-agent-run-invoke

item.title: DiskANN Vector Index Improvements
item.description: Remember when we announced the Public Preview of DiskANN vector indexes back in November and mentioned that once you created the index, your table became readâ€‘only? Yeahâ Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sql/diskann-vector-index-improvements

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.112
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.112 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.112

item.title: VS Code 1.112 Insiders
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.112 (Insiders) Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/external-blogs/vs-code-1-112-insiders

item.title: Handling Long-Running Operations with Background Responses
item.description: Handling Long-Running Operations with Background Responses AI agents powered by reasoning models can take minutes to work through complex problems — deep research, multi-step analysis, lengthy content generation. In a traditional request-response pattern, that means your client sits idle waiting for a connection that may time out, Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/handling-long-running-operations-with-background-responses

item.title: When Infrastructure Scales But Understanding Doesn't
item.description: We all know this, even if we don't like to admit it: modern infrastructure can scale infinitely, but human understanding doesn't. We've all seen it happen - organizations going from managing dozens of servers to thousands of containers, from deploying weekly to deploying hundreds of times per day, from serving thousands of users to millions Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/all-things-azure/when-infrastructure-scales-but-understanding-doesnt

item.title: From 150 Unread to Zero Stress: Automating Inbox Triage with MCP and GitHub Copilot
item.description: Taming the Noisy Inbox: How I Used MCP to Automate Email and Teams Triage How the Model Context Protocol (MCP) turns your AI coding assistant into a workplace productivity engine — connecting Microsoft 365 data to your terminal workflow. The Problem We All Share If you work in a customer-facing role, y Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/all-things-azure/from-150-unread-to-zero-stress-automating-inbox-triage-with-mcp-and-github-copilot

item.title: Azure Skills Plugin - Let's Get Started!
item.description: Part 2 of the Azure Skills Plugin series Previously: Announcing the Azure Skills Plugin This post is all about getting you up and running. I won't go d Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/all-things-azure/azure-skills-plugin-lets-get-started

item.title: PowerToys 0.98 is here: new Keyboard Manager UX, the Command Palette Dock and better CursorWrap!
item.description: Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/powertoys-0-98-is-here-new-keyboard-manager-ux-the-command-palette-dock-and-better-cursorwrap

item.title: RT.Assistant: A Multi-Agent Voice Bot Using .NET and OpenAI
item.description: This is a guest post by Faisal Waris, an AI strategist in the telecom industry. Faisal built RT.Assistant to explore how .NET, F#, and the OpenAI Realtime API can come together in a production-style, multi-agent voice application. RT.Assistant i Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/rt-assistant-a-realtime-multiagent-voice-bot-using-dotnet-and-open-ai-api

item.title: Introducing the Azure Cosmos DB Plugin for Cursor
item.description: We're excited to announce the Cursor plugin for Azure Cosmos DB bringing AI-powered database expertise, best practices guidance, and live database connectivity directly into your coding workflow. Whether you're designing a new data model, optimizing queries, or building a RAG appl Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/introducing-the-azure-cosmos-db-plugin-for-cursor

item.title: Windows stack limit checking retrospective: x86-32 also known as i386, second try
item.description: The last time we looked at the Windows stack limit checker on x86-32 (also known as i386), we noted that the function has changed over the years. Here's the revised version. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260317-00/?p=112144

item.title: Azure DevOps Remote MCP Server (public preview)
item.description: When we released the local Azure DevOps MCP Server, it gave customers a way to connect Azure DevOps data with tools like Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code through GitHub Copilot Chat. The next step was to make this experience easier to get started with and to enable it for servi Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/azure-devops-remote-mcp-server-public-preview
